data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-30T01:36:28.545Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating this .NET Framework code to .NET 8 and need to ensure all Thread.Abort
usage is addressed. Check this code and provide your Thread.Abort migration guidance.

```csharp
public class SimpleWorker
{
    public void RunBatch(List<WorkItem> items)
    {
        var threads = new List<Thread>();

        foreach (var item in items)
        {
            var t = new Thread(() => Process(item));
            t.Start();
            threads.Add(t);
        }

        foreach (var t in threads)
            t.Join();
    }

    private void Process(WorkItem item)
    {
        // simulate work
        Thread.Sleep(100);
        item.Result = ComputeResult(item.Input);
    }
}
```

data.content:
Your code **does not use `Thread.Abort`**, `Thread.Interrupt`, `ThreadAbortException`, or `Thread.ResetAbort`. It only uses `Thread.Start`, `Thread.Join`, and `Thread.Sleep` — all of which work identically in .NET 8 and require **no migration**.

That said, the code could be modernized to use `Task`/`Parallel.ForEach` with `CancellationToken` for better resource usage and cancellation support, but that's a general modernization concern, not a `Thread.Abort` migration issue.

**No changes needed** for your .NET 8 migration regarding thread abort.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about migrating .NET Framework code that uses Thread.Abort to .NET 8. Let me look at the code they provided - it actually doesn't use Thread.Abort at all. It just uses Thread.Start, Thread.Join, and Thread.Sleep.

There's a skill available called "thread-abort-migration" that seems relevant. Let me check the skill description again:

"Guides migration of .NET Framework Thread.Abort usage to cooperative cancellation in modern .NET. USE FOR: modernizing code that calls Thread.Abort, catching ThreadAbortException, replacing Thread.ResetAbort, replacing Thread.Interrupt for thread termination, resolving PlatformNotSupportedException or SYSLIB0006 after retargeting to .NET 6+... DO NOT USE FOR: code that only uses Thread.Join, Thread.Sleep, or Thread.Start without any abort, interrupt, or ThreadAbortException usage — these APIs work identically in modern .NET and need no migration."


The code only uses Thread.Start, Thread.Join, and Thread.Sleep, which are all fully supported in .NET 8 without any changes needed. I shouldn't invoke the skill since this code doesn't actually use Thread.Abort or any of the problematic APIs that require migration.
